description: We’re the Driver and Vehicle Licensing Agency (DVLA), maintaining nearly 53 million driver records and more than 47 million vehicle records. Last year (2024 to 2025), we collected £8.4 billion in Vehicle Excise Duty (VED). DVLA is an executive agency, sponsored by the Department for Transport .

details.body:
We’re the Driver and Vehicle Licensing Agency (DVLA), maintaining nearly 53 million driver records and more than 47 million vehicle records. Last year (2024 to 2025), we collected £8.4 billion in Vehicle Excise Duty (VED).

DVLA is an executive agency, sponsored by the Department for Transport.

ordered_featured_documents.summary: DVLA has launched a new campaign featuring a talking clamp to remind drivers to tax their vehicles and underlines the consequences of failing to do so.
ordered_featured_documents.title: DVLA warns ‘nobody wins’ as new campaign targets untaxed vehicles

ordered_featured_documents.summary: New changes will enable bus, coach and lorry drivers with diabetes to use Continuous Glucose Monitoring Systems.
ordered_featured_documents.title: DVLA modernises diabetes rules for bus and lorry drivers

ordered_featured_documents.summary: DVLA announces new policies for registering repaired, restored and modified vehicles that will come into effect from Tuesday 26 August.
ordered_featured_documents.title: DVLA backs classic car industry and driving enthusiasts with updated registration policies

ordered_featured_documents.summary: Tips for motorists on how to recognise and avoid scams, including emails and text messages.
ordered_featured_documents.title: DVLA’s top tips for avoiding scams

ordered_featured_documents.summary: How the Vehicle Excise Duty (VED) changes affect your vehicle.
ordered_featured_documents.title: Vehicle tax for electric, zero and low emission vehicles

ordered_featured_documents.summary: This explains the Driver and Vehicle Licensing Agency’s performance, achievements and financial statements for the financial year 1 April 2024 to 31 March 2025.
ordered_featured_documents.title: DVLA annual report and accounts 2024 to 2025

ordered_featured_links.title: Check if a vehicle is taxed and has an MOT
Tell DVLA you've changed your address

details.secondary_corporate_information_pages: Read about the types of information we routinely publish in our Publication scheme. Find out about our commitment to publishing in Welsh. Our Personal information charter explains how we treat your personal information. Read our policy on Social media use. Find out About our services.

role_appointments.title: Tim Moss - Chief Executive

details.body:
The chief executive is the leader of the IPO and is responsible for the day-to-day management of the organisation.

The Comptroller General is formally responsible for the operation of the IPO and supervising the quality of its accounting and financial reporting.

role.title: Chief Executive and Comptroller General, IPO

role_appointments.title: Tim Moss - Chief Executive and Comptroller General, IPO

details.body:
The Chief Executive and Registrar for England and Wales is responsible for:

  • incorporating and registering companies
  • taking action if a company fails to file the documents required under the Companies Act 2006, which may involve dissolving companies or penalising directors
  • making company data available to the public
  • the day to day running of Companies House

role_appointments.title: Tim Moss - Chief Executive, Registrar of Companies

details.body: The CTO is responsible for IT in DVLA – the day-to-day operational business of running both the internal business systems, as well as the external customer facing services. IT also includes implementing new technical projects and programmes to improve the Agency, as well looking at the long term IT strategy for new technology adoption and innovation.

role_appointments.title: Brian Sullivan - Chief Technology Officer

details.body: Responsible for providing commercial, procurement and business development leadership at a strategic and operational level, reducing non-staff costs and seeking opportunities to market and develop DVLA services.

details.body: Responsible for providing commercial, procurement and business development leadership at a strategic and operational level, reducing non-staff costs and seeking opportunities to market and develop DVLA services.

role_appointments.title: Darren Thompson - Commercial Director

details.body: The Operations and Customer Services Director has responsibility for the creation and accurate maintenance of around 100 million driver and vehicle records. The role focuses on the delivery of high quality customer services with a strong emphasis on increasing the availability of digital channels. Efficiency of operational delivery is also a key driver with value for money at the forefront of planning and decision making.

role_appointments.title: Tony Ackroyd - Operations and Customer Services Director

details.body: Responsible for providing financial leadership at a strategic and operational level, supporting the wider business whilst ensuring sound and appropriate governance and risk management controls are in place across the organisation.

role_appointments.title: Tracy Nash - Interim Finance Director

details.body: The responsibility for providing financial and procurement leadership at a strategic and operational level, ensuring sound and appropriate governance and risk management controls are in place across the organisation.

role_appointments.title: Tracy Nash - Finance and Assurance Services Director

details.body: The Strategy and Policy Director has responsibility for the DVLA business strategy and for policy development as well as internal and external communications. The Strategy and Policy Directorate is responsible for the development of the DVLA business strategy and for creating sound policies for delivering excellent services to DVLA customers. It’s also responsible for advising ministers honestly, impartially and objectively to allow them to make informed choices about DVLA strategy and policy, using evidence and understanding of the practical implications of delivery.

role_appointments.title: Lynette Rose - Strategy, Policy & Communications Director

details.body: Responsible for the overall leadership of DVLA.

details.body:
The HR and Estates Director has overall responsibility for the provision of HR and estates management services to DVLA. The role is a key member of both DVLA’s Executive team and Agency Board, having a strong understanding of the agency’s strategic objectives, their people and estate implications.

The role provides leadership to ensure high performing and engaged colleagues, a thriving organisational culture and an estate which supports DVLA’s operational delivery and transformation agenda.

role_appointments.title: Sarah-Ellen Stacey - HR and Estates Director

details.body: The responsibility for providing financial and procurement leadership at a strategic and operational level, ensuring sound and appropriate governance and risk management controls are in place across the organisation.

The Chief Operating Officer holds responsibility for all DVLA’s operational areas which underpin the creation and maintenance of accurate Driver and Vehicle records. The role focuses on the continuous development and delivery of high quality customer services with a strong emphasis on increasing the availability of digital channels. Efficiency of operational delivery is also a key driver with value for money at the forefront of planning and decision making.
Responsible for all DVLA IT services and development programmes delivered through an internal team and an outsourced contract with IBM.

Responsible for all DVLA’s operational areas, focusing on the development and implementation of an operational and customer focussed strategy whilst meeting public service targets, improving accurancy, productivity, efficiency and performance in the short, medium and long term.

The HR and Estates Director has overall responsibility for the provision of HR and estates management services to DVLA. The role is a key member of both DVLA’s Executive team and Agency Board, having a strong understanding of the agency’s strategic objectives, their people and estate implications.

The role provides leadership to ensure high performing and engaged colleagues, a thriving organisational culture and an estate which supports DVLA’s operational delivery and transformation agenda.

The Strategy and Policy Director has responsibility for the DVLA business strategy and for policy development as well as internal and external communications. The Strategy and Policy Directorate is responsible for the development of the DVLA business strategy and for creating sound policies for delivering excellent services to DVLA customers. It’s also responsible for advising ministers honestly, impartially and objectively to allow them to make informed choices about DVLA strategy and policy, using evidence and understanding of the practical implications of delivery.
Responsible for the alignment and coordination of change to achieve the government’s strategic direction.
Responsible for bringing ideas and advice, both from his experience of the wider business world and from his perspective as a private citizen.
Responsible for the overall leadership of DVLA.

The CTO is responsible for IT in DVLA – the day-to-day operational business of running both the internal business systems, as well as the external customer facing services. IT also includes implementing new technical projects and programmes to improve the Agency, as well looking at the long term IT strategy for new technology adoption and innovation.
Responsible for providing ideas and advice on the strategies and operations, from her experience of both the automotive industry and broader digital business markets.

The Operations and Customer Services Director has responsibility for the creation and accurate maintenance of around 100 million driver and vehicle records. The role focuses on the delivery of high quality customer services with a strong emphasis on increasing the availability of digital channels. Efficiency of operational delivery is also a key driver with value for money at the forefront of planning and decision making.
Responsible for bringing ideas and advice, both from his experience of the wider business world and from his perspective as a private citizen.

Responsible for providing commercial, procurement and business development leadership at a strategic and operational level, reducing non-staff costs and seeking opportunities to market and develop DVLA services.
Responsible for bringing ideas and advice, both from his experience of the wider business world and from his perspective as a private citizen.
Responsible for bringing ideas and advice, both from his experience of the wider business world and his perspective as a private citizen.
